summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including the development of new AI models, improved AI-powered tools, and increased adoption of AI in various industries. However, concerns about AI safety, security, and ethics continue to grow.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is creating new opportunities for innovation and growth, but it also raises important questions about the potential risks and consequences of relying on AI systems.
highlights: Google is expanding its AI Mode to let users link and interact with select apps
OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections and learning tools
NVIDIA is introducing new Jetson Thor computers to advance mainstream robotics and edge AI

articles.title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
articles.description: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
articles.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

articles.title: Google-backed satellites for wildfire detection launch as smoke chokes US, Canada
articles.description: The FireSat program can spot wildfires that other satellites miss.

articles.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
